mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.77% as of 2007-11-15 indicates aggressive easing cycle
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following credit shock
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window during easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate above 4.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% as of 2007-11-15 but rising trend expected in credit crisis
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 672,874M as of 2007-11-15 in credit crisis environment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances following RSAFS declines exceeding 2% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 6-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction signals reduced economic capacity utilization and corporate profitability deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.60 as of 2007-11-15 showing early signs of contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO declines consecutively, or Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals synchronized demand collapse across residential and consumer sectors, amplifying negative equity returns through multiplier effects and credit contagion, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,500K units and RSAFS declines month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,724K and RSAFS at 672,874M as of 2007-11-15 in housing crisis
regime_hint: synchronized demand shock across housing and consumer sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both HOUST < 1,500K and RSAFS declines, or interaction effect (joint signal) produces lower Rank IC than either signal alone.

mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces aggregate income and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through reduced earnings growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151,682K and UNRATE at 3.6% as of 2007-11-15
regime_hint: labor market recession and employment contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monetary easing (FEDFUNDS decline) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals stagflationary pressure that creates policy uncertainty and equity risk premium expansion, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices tail risks.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines more than 0.5pp while CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.77% and CPIAUCSL at 291.47 as of 2007-11-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines and CPIAUCSL rises, or interaction term adds no explanatory power beyond individual series (R² increase < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) contraction signals recession onset that compresses corporate earnings multiples and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism is regime-conditional: it is most powerful in late-cycle expansions where growth deceleration triggers sharp multiple compression.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,731B (2017$) as of 2007-10-15 in late-cycle expansion
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion transitioning to recession with earnings multiple compression
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window in late-cycle regimes.
